sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Patient reports anxiety regarding recent hepatitis C blood work results and a desire for clarification.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports being surprised by the hepatitis C diagnosis, despite having no prior knowledge of it. He states he has been feeling well and reports a history of occasional heavy alcohol consumption in the past, having been clean for over fifteen years. He admits to smoking one to two cigarettes per day, having previously smoked a pack and a half daily. The patient reports experiencing occasional fatigue, particularly at the end of the workday, related to increased work demands. He also reports a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression. He has been busy at work recently, but not significantly more so than usual.
Findings
Physical exam revealed a regular rate and rhythm heart, with a two out of six systolic murmur appreciated. Lung sounds were clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi. The abdomen was soft with normal bowel sounds and no hepatosplenomegaly. Musculoskeletal exam was unremarkable, with no gait disturbance or edema. Liver function tests showed an elevated AST of 39 U/L, with normal ALT and bilirubin levels. Hepatitis C antibody testing was positive.
Assessment
The patient has been diagnosed with hepatitis C, requiring further investigation and management.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I will order hepatitis C RNA testing and an HCV genotype to confirm the diagnosis. A liver ultrasound will be ordered to assess for liver fibrosis. I will schedule the patient for follow-up in three weeks to discuss the results and determine the appropriate treatment plan. I will also recommend screening for his wife and children for hepatitis C.
